display.title: Santos y Misterios as Channels of Communication in the Diaspora: Afro-Dominican Religious Practices Abroad
display.publisher: University of Illinois Press on behalf of American Folklore Society
display.abstract: Whereas most Afro-Caribbean Creole religious practices have been the focus of scholarly studies for some time, such practices in the Dominican Republic have received considerably less attention. Drawing on ethnographic fieldwork among Dominican women in Madrid and the southwestern Dominican Republic, this article presents the saints of the Dominican Vodou pantheon as actual communication agents who enable transnational practitioners to maintain communication and to solve migration-related proble

marc.label: Title
marc.value: Santos y Misterios as Channels of Communication in the Diaspora: Afro-Dominican Religious Practices Abroad

marc.label: Published
marc.value: University of Illinois Press on behalf of American Folklore Society, 2005.
